data.title: Michael Saylor: Shadow Banking Activities Suppress Bitcoin's Price
data.description: Michael Saylor has highlighted that Bitcoin's struggle to surpass the $126,000 mark is significantly impacted by shadow banking activities. According to NS3.AI, traditional banks' exclusion of Bitcoin drives investors towards shadow banks, which engage in rehypothecation of collateral. This process artificially increases selling pressure, thereby suppressing Bitcoin's price by expanding supply pressure beyond the actual demand.

data.title: Bitcoin Derivatives Open Interest Hits 30-Day High of $24.7 Billion
data.description: Bitcoin derivatives open interest has surged to $24.7 billion, marking the highest level in the past 30 days. According to NS3.AI, this rise indicates increased trader activity and interest in Bitcoin futures and options. Market participants may be preparing for potential price movements or volatility.

data.title: Walmart to Pay $100 Million in Settlement Over Misleading Driver Pay Claims
data.description: Walmart has agreed to a $100 million settlement to resolve allegations from the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and 11 states that it misled delivery drivers about their pay and tips through its Spark Driver app. According to NS3.AI, the lawsuit claimed that Walmart misrepresented driver earnings and the distribution of tips, resulting in drivers losing millions over a four-year period. As part of the settlement, Walmart will allocate $79 million to compensate drivers and pay $10 million to the FTC, with the remaining amount distributed among the participating states.

data.title: Challenges in Private BDCs' Market Pricing
data.description: Private Business Development Companies (BDCs) face structural challenges that hinder their ability to trade at market-clearing prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these issues stem from the inherent design of private BDCs, which affects their market performance. The structure of these financial entities often leads to discrepancies in pricing, preventing them from aligning with market expectations. This misalignment can impact investors and the overall market dynamics, raising concerns about the efficiency of private BDCs in the financial ecosystem.

data.title: Russia Considers Shifting Natural Gas Sales Away from Europe
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced that Russia is contemplating a shift in its natural gas sales strategy, potentially reducing its supply to Europe in favor of exploring more promising markets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Putin's remarks on the strategic realignment of Russia's energy exports. This move comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic considerations, as Russia seeks to diversify its energy partnerships and reduce dependency on European markets. The decision could have significant implications for global energy dynamics and European energy security, prompting discussions on alternative energy sources and supply chain adjustments.

data.title: White House Confirms Ground Troops Not Part of Iran Strategy
data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt stated that ground troops are currently not included in the operational plans concerning Iran. According to Jin10, this announcement comes amid ongoing discussions about the U.S. military's strategic approach in the region. The statement aims to clarify the U.S. position and address speculations about potential military escalations involving Iran. The administration continues to evaluate its options while emphasizing diplomatic efforts to manage tensions.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Discusses Military Base with Kurdish Leader
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has engaged in a conversation with a Kurdish leader regarding the U.S. military base located in northern Iraq. According to Jin10, the discussion focused on the strategic importance of the base and its role in regional stability. The dialogue underscores ongoing diplomatic efforts to address security concerns in the area. The White House has not disclosed further details about the conversation or any potential outcomes from the discussion.

data.title: New York Governor Hochul Loses Ground in Tightening Race
data.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul has seen a decline in her poll numbers, losing six points to her Republican opponent, Bruce Blakeman, as the gubernatorial race becomes more competitive. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shift in voter sentiment as the election approaches. The tightening race indicates a growing challenge for Hochul, who has been working to maintain her lead in the face of increasing support for Blakeman. The political landscape in New York is becoming more dynamic, with both candidates intensifying their campaigns to sway undecided voters.

data.title: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang to Invest $30 Billion in OpenAI
data.description: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang has announced plans to invest $30 billion in OpenAI. According to Jin10, Huang clarified that a $100 billion investment is not currently under consideration. This move highlights NVIDIA's commitment to advancing artificial intelligence technologies and strengthening its partnership with OpenAI. The investment is expected to enhance research and development efforts, fostering innovation in AI applications. Huang's statement underscores the strategic importance of AI in NVIDIA's future growth plans.

data.title: White House to Announce Measures for Americans Returning from Middle East
data.description: The White House is set to unveil additional measures in the coming days to assist Americans returning from the Middle East. According to Jin10, White House Press Secretary Levitt stated that these steps aim to facilitate the safe and efficient return of U.S. citizens amid ongoing regional challenges. The announcement underscores the administration's commitment to ensuring the well-being of its nationals abroad. Further details on the specific measures and their implementation are expected to be disclosed soon.

data.title: U.S. Department of Justice Investigates Fertilizer Market for Price Manipulation
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is currently investigating potential price manipulation within the American fertilizer market. According to Jin10, this inquiry aims to determine whether any unfair practices have influenced market prices, impacting both producers and consumers. The investigation reflects growing concerns over market transparency and fairness in the agricultural sector. Further details on the scope and findings of the investigation are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: DeFi Sector Faces Challenges as MEV Capital's Assets Drop 80%
data.description:
MEV Capital has seen a significant decline in its assets under management, dropping by 80% from $1.5 billion to $300 million. According to NS3.AI, this downturn is attributed to exposure to leveraged yield strategies involving deUSD, which lost its peg following the collapse of Stream Finance. The broader DeFi yield vault sector has been affected, experiencing a $4 billion value wipeout due to recursive lending failures.

Many projects within the sector are struggling to regain investor confidence. While some institutions are shifting focus towards tokenized real-world assets, there are ongoing concerns about off-chain risks affecting on-chain DeFi collateral. The situation highlights the challenges faced by the DeFi sector in maintaining stability and investor trust amidst market volatility.

data.title: Private Credit Turmoil May Persist Into Next Year, Says Apollo Executive
data.description: John Zito, an executive at Apollo, expressed concerns that the challenges facing private credit markets may continue into next year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Zito's remarks about the ongoing issues in the sector. Despite the turbulence, Zito minimized the significance of withdrawal waves affecting certain funds, suggesting they are not a major concern. The private credit market has been experiencing volatility, raising questions about its stability and future prospects.

data.title: Explosions Reported in Multiple Iranian Cities
data.description: Explosions have been reported in several Iranian cities, including northern Tehran, central Yazd, and northwestern Tabriz. According to Jin10, local media sources have confirmed the incidents, though details remain scarce. Authorities are investigating the cause and impact of these explosions. No casualties or significant damage have been reported at this time. The situation is developing, and further updates are expected as more information becomes available.

data.title: Bitcoin Rallies 9% Following Pro-Bitcoin Federal Reserve Nomination
data.description: The White House has nominated Kevin Warsh as the new Federal Reserve Chairman, a decision perceived as favorable for Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, this announcement triggered a 9% surge in Bitcoin's price within 12 hours, resulting in over $530 million in liquidated short positions. Ethereum also experienced an 11% increase. The nomination has prompted a shift in capital from traditional safe havens like gold to digital assets, indicating growing macroeconomic and institutional support for cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Hong Kong Conference Highlights Institutional Shift in Crypto Market
data.description: Consensus Hong Kong 2026 highlighted the crypto market's transition towards long-term institutional allocation. According to NS3.AI, this shift is fueled by increased adoption of exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and evolving soft regulations in the United States. Key discussions at the conference focused on the necessity for institutional-grade infrastructure, robust regulatory frameworks, and advancements in stablecoins and tokenization, especially in Hong Kong. The event emphasized that institutional risk appetite is contingent upon reliable, compliant, and scalable market infrastructure.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump to Endorse Republican Candidate in Texas Senate Runoff
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to endorse the Republican candidate in the upcoming Texas Senate runoff election. According to Jin10, Trump urged candidates who have not received his endorsement to withdraw from the race. This move is seen as an effort to consolidate support within the Republican Party and strengthen its position in the Senate. The endorsement is expected to influence the dynamics of the election, as Trump's backing often carries significant weight among Republican voters. The Texas Senate runoff is a critical contest that could impact the balance of power in the Senate.

data.title: White House Press Secretary Levitt: Iranian Regime Under Pressure
data.description: White House Press Secretary Levitt stated that the Iranian regime is currently facing significant pressure. According to Jin10, this development comes amid increasing international scrutiny and domestic challenges. The situation in Iran has been a focal point of geopolitical discussions, with various factors contributing to the regime's current predicament. Observers are closely monitoring how these pressures might influence Iran's political landscape and its interactions on the global stage.
